info.title: LazyLibrarian
info.description:
LazyLibrarian JSON API.

All commands are dispatched through a single endpoint:

    GET /api?apikey=<key>&cmd=<command>&<params...>

Each command is documented here as its own path item keyed `/api?cmd=<command>`; the actual request is always `GET /api` with `cmd` and `apikey` as query parameters. Parameter values are `quote_plus`-encoded (spaces become `+`). Time-consuming commands return `OK` immediately and run in the background unless `&wait` is supplied. A JSONP `&callback=` wrapper is available on every command.

Authentication is via the `apikey` query parameter. A separate read-only key may be configured; commands marked `x-ll-requires-full-key: true` require the full key.

Generated from the LazyLibrarian source `lazylibrarian/api.py`.

tags.name: Authors
tags.description: Author records: add, remove, refresh, lock, image, follow-state.

tags.name: Books
tags.description: Book records: add, queue, move, cover, metadata, OPF, listings.

tags.name: Magazines
tags.description: Magazines and their issues, covers, tags.

tags.name: Comics
tags.description: Comic identification, metadata, search and scan.

tags.name: Library
tags.description: Library scans, imports and download post-processing.

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Book/magazine/RSS/wishlist searches and RSS feed output.

tags.name: System
tags.description: Version, jobs, stats, logs, lifecycle, backup, telemetry.

apikey.name: apikey
apikey.description: 32-character API key. A read-only key may also be configured; commands flagged x-ll-requires-full-key require the full key.

callback.name: callback
callback.description: JSONP callback function name; wraps the response.

Data.description: Command-specific payload (object, array, string, or empty).

Config.description: The full LazyLibrarian configuration, grouped by config.ini section. There is no bulk config endpoint — individual values are read/written with `cmd=readCFG` / `cmd=writeCFG` (`name` = the variable key, `group` = the section). Fields marked readOnly are runtime-only (not persisted). Parsed from `lazylibrarian/configdefs.py` `BASE_DEFAULTS`.

SKIPPED_EXT.default: fail, part, bts, !ut, torrent, magnet, nzb, unpack

ABB_DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

KAT_DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

TPB_DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

TDL_DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

BOK_DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

SLSK_DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

ANNA_DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

LIME_DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

MAG_NOUNS.default: winter, spring, summer, fall, autumn, christmas, edition, special

ConfigNewznab.description: A single 'NEWZNAB' provider array entry (config.ini section NEWZNAB_<n>).

DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

ConfigTorznab.description: A single 'TORZNAB' provider array entry (config.ini section TORZNAB_<n>).

DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

ConfigRss.description: A single 'RSS' provider array entry (config.ini section RSS_<n>).

DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

ConfigIrc.description: A single 'IRC' provider array entry (config.ini section IRC_<n>).

DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

ConfigGen.description: A single 'GEN' provider array entry (config.ini section GEN_<n>).

DLTYPES.description: Comma-separated download types (A=audio, E=ebook, M=magazine, C=comic).

ConfigApprise.description: A single 'APPRISE' provider array entry (config.ini section APPRISE_<n>).

TextOk.description: Plain-text result. Simple commands return `OK`; background commands return `OK` immediately (add `&wait` to block). Some commands return a JSON payload here instead.

IssueArray.description: List of issues; when `name` is given, an object `{magazine, issues}` is returned instead.

BinaryFile.description: The requested file is streamed as an attachment.

Unauthorized.description: Missing/incorrect API key, API disabled, or command not permitted with a read-only key.
